summary: This page concludes an example on sketching the exponentially damped sinusoid 4e^{-2t}cos(6t-60°), explaining how the exponential terms ±4e^{-2t} form upper and lower envelopes for the waveform. It then begins Section B.4 on Cramer's Rule, introducing a system of n simultaneous linear equations in n unknowns and noting that these equations can be written in matrix form.
